prompts.prompt_text: Your Life Story - Write your life story in 1,000 characters or less, as though it were the table of contents for the book of Your Life.
What job would you like to have immediately upon graduating with an MBA? (Two-year program)
How did your pre-MBA experience prepare you for the job you envision post-MBA? (One-year program)
Optional essay to address any aspects of your application you feel need further explanation or to highlight your strengths

essay_prompts: Through your resume and recommendations, we have a clear sense of your professional path to date. What are your career goals over the next three to five years and what is your long-term dream job? (500 words)
Please share a specific example of how you made a team more collaborative, more inclusive or fostered a greater sense of community within an organization. (250 words)

We believe Columbia Business School is a special place with a collaborative learning environment in which students feel a sense of belonging, agency, and partnership—academically, culturally, and professionally. How would you co-create your optimal MBA experience at CBS? Please be specific. (250 words)
data.key_differentiators: Columbia's primary advantage is its Manhattan location, providing access to Wall Street and thousands of multinational corporations as a 'living laboratory' for business students. The school offers both traditional MBA and accelerated January-entry programs, with the J-term option being somewhat less competitive for admission.

essay_prompts: Business-Minded Essay: Please reflect on how your choices have influenced your career path and aspirations. (300 words)
Leadership-Focused Essay: What experiences have shaped how you invest in others and how you lead? (250 words)
Growth-Oriented Essay: Curiosity can be seen in many ways. Please share an example of how you have demonstrated curiosity and how that has influenced your growth. (up to 250 words)
data.key_differentiators: Harvard Business School is the home of the case method of business education and has one of the largest and most influential alumni networks in the world, with 20% of the top three executive positions at Fortune 500 companies filled by HBS alumni. Students become part of smaller community 'sections' of about ninety students with dedicated faculty teams for a more intimate learning experience within the larger program.

MIT Sloan is a highly academic school that prides itself on parlaying theory into practical application, drawing from MIT's world-renowned science and engineering programs to bolster its intellectual culture. The school emphasizes a hands-on approach perfect for creating revolutionary inventions through its Entrepreneurship Center, which hosts a $100,000 competition annually for the best new business plan.

The page mentions Wharton has group interviews as part of their admissions process, suggesting a collaborative evaluation approach that may reflect the school's emphasis on teamwork and interpersonal skills.
